results.content: Home » Japan » The Ultimate Japan Bucket List: 30 Unmissable Experiences I left my corporate career of nearly a decade in finance to become a full-time travel blogger. And, guess what? Japan is the country that made me fall in love with travel. From my very first trip in 2013, as an unseasoned traveller at the tender age of 21 years old, I fell madly in love with the country, the food, the culture and the experiences. It’s kept me coming back over and over. Fast forward to today, with six trips under my belt, I like to think I know a thing or two about travelling in Japan. After reflecting on all these wonderful Japan adventures, I’ve prepared this list of the top 30 must-do and must-sees that are worthy of being on your Japan bucket list. These are all things that I’ve done personally in Japan, and I think you will love too. Table of Contents (click to expand) On my fifth trip to Japan, I finally got to marvel at this iconic view that has been a symbol of Japan, splashed across postca

Clickbait, spam, memes, ads/selling/buying, brochures, referrals, classifieds, surveys or self-promotion will be removed. I know Tokyo is a must, and I’m planning on dedicating about 6 days to it. Does this seem reasonable? And as far as what I’m looking to do, a little bit of everything. Japanese culture and history fascinates me, I love anime, good food as well, and I also enjoy being outside (riding bikes, swimming, hiking, etc). Im thinking Kyoto is another good stop because of its historical and cultural significance but looking to hear what others have to say. And I’m an experienced traveler so don’t have a problem going off the beaten path. Anyone have advice? Traveling with my wife who has similar interests to me if it matters. Create your account an
